title: Box Office: 'Abigail' to Take on 'Civil War' With $12 Million Debut
text: The vampire thriller Abigail is positioned to claim the top spot at the domestic box office with projected earnings of 12 million to 15 million from 3,300 North American theaters. However, it faces competition from A24's Civil War, which dominated last weekend. The provocative thriller, which debuted last weekend with $25.8 million, is expected to bring in between $10 million to $12 million in its sophomore outing. From Universal Pictures, this R-rated horror film reimagines the 1936 classic Dracula's Daughter. The plot follows kidnappers who capture a young ballerina and demand a $50 million ransom, only to discover she's actually a vampire child who hunts them down. Directors Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett (known as Radio Silence) helmed the project, which was produced on a $28 million budget. Critics have embraced the film, which holds a 82% on Rotten Tomatoes — basically a rave for horror. Other newcomers like Guy Ritchie's The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare and the anime feature Spy x Family Code: White are targeting more modest openings around $5 million to $6 million.
